Literature Title: An Improved Healthcare Accessibility Measure Considering the Temporal Dimension and Population Demand of Different Ages
Demand Population: Communities' population (median centers used as population locations)

Literature Title: Assessing Spatial Accessibility to Medical Resources at the Community Level in Shenzhen, China
Accessibility Method: shortest path analysis, Gini coefficient, and enhanced 2-step floating catchment area (E2SFCA)
Facility Type: Public hospitals, Private general hospitals, Beds, Doctors, Nurses, Maternity units
Dist/Time Calc Method: OD cost matrix, travel time thresholds

Literature Title: Modelling geographic accessibility to Primary Health Care Facilities: combining open data and geospatial analysis
Accessibility Method: Terrain attributes and distance from road network combined with known location of PHCF
Facility Type: primary, secondary and tertiary health care service facilities
Demand Population: 96% of the population in good access class areas, 4% in poor and very poor access regions
Dist/Time Calc Method: Speed model created using conditions in Table 1 for calculating the travel time from each PHCF

Literature Title: Spatio-temporal patterns of healthservice delivery and access to maternal, child, and outpatient healthcare in Volta region, Ghana: a repeated cross-sectional ecological study using health facility data
Accessibility Method: spatial demographic data, transportation, and health facility databases
Facility Type: tertiary, secondary and primary healthcare, private, charitable and publicly funded facilities
Demand Population: mothers and children under 5 years, relative to the general population
Dist/Time Calc Method: Multimodal walking and mechanised travel time model
Transport Mode: Moderate walking speed in travel time

Literature Title: The Contributions of Population Distribution, Healthcare Resourcing, and Transportation Infrastructure to Spatial Accessibility of Health Care
Study Area & Country: Chishui county from Guizhou province, China
Accessibility Method: Two-Step Floating Catchment Area (2SFCA), Enhanced Two-Step Floating Catchment Area (E2SFCA), Enhanced ThreeStep Floating Catchment Area (E3SFCA), Huff model
Facility Type: General hospitals, specialist hospitals, township health centers, village clinics
Demand Population: more than 3 million residents living in impoverished counties
Dist/Time Calc Method: specific speed for different types of roads, walking speed for short distances
Transport Mode: traveling out of town, poor marked roads, limited public transportation, inhospitable terrain or weather

Literature Title: Evaluating spatial accessibility to healthcare services under travel time uncertainty: A reliability-based floating catchment area approach
Accessibility Method: reliability-based two-step foating catchment area (2SFCA) method
Demand Population: Population in Luohu and Futian districts; very low demand in some areas
Dist/Time Calc Method: Distance decay function (Gaussian, exponential, inverse power, kernel density)

Literature Title: Spatial Accessibility to Primary Healthcare Services by Multimodal Means of Travel: Synthesis and Case Study in the City of Calgary
Dist/Time Calc Method: Simple conversion of distance to time for sidewalks, trails, and pathways; conversion from distance to time for bus routes and road network using speed limits
Transport Mode: Multimodal means of travel, Walking, Driving

Literature Title: Disparities in Geographic Access to Pediatric Subspecialty Care
Study Area & Country: West North Central and Mountain regions, small metropolitan and non-metropolitan areas, United States
Accessibility Method: Average distance to pediatric subspecialty care

Study Area & Country: Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada; Nunavut, Canada; Ontario, Canada
Demand Population: residents of five Canadian provinces and territories

Literature Title: To examine NZ physiotherapy spatial accessibility at a high acuity level in relation to demographic indicators of health need (M¯aori or Pacifc ethnicity, socioeconomic deprivation, rurality)
Study Area & Country: NZ, Canada, South Australia, United States
Accessibility Method: distance-based, three step floating catchment area (3SFCA) geographic information systems (GIS) methodology
Facility Type: physiotherapy, GP, chiropractic, osteopathic, podiatry, acupuncture
Demand Population: rural, low socioeconomic deprivation, high socioeconomic deprivation, indigenous Maori, Pacific peoples, high deprivation
